title: vernonia greggii subsp. greggii
intro: Vernonia greggii subsp. greggii, also known as the Gray Goldeneye, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antifungal activities. Vernonia greggii subsp. greggii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health issues.
